data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260511T013249-1c092fb4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260511T013249-1c092fb4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260511T013249-1c092fb4/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260511T013249-1c092fb4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260511T013249-1c092fb4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-4335a1e9">
    <title>feat(review): skip reviewer dispatch when implementer produced no commits</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  When an implementer attempt produces no_changes (BaseRev ==
  ResultRev, no commits), the drain still dispatches the reviewer
  agent. The reviewer is paid for an empty-diff review; it
  contributes nothing because there are no changes to evaluate.
  This is pure cost waste — the no_changes triage path (existing,
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go around the no_changes
  outcome handling) already routes the bead correctly without
  needing a reviewer signal.

  Per ADR-024 P1 (cheapest first), the reviewer's cost on an
  empty-diff dispatch is unjustified. Cost optimization includes
  "skip the unnecessary".

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: when implementer
    returns no_changes, the dispatch flow proceeds to reviewer
    invocation regardless.
  - No early-exit / lazy-dispatch logic.
  - Reviewer cost: typically smart-tier, $0.30-1.00 per invocation.
  - Multiplied across N no_changes attempts per bead (N=2-3 in
    smart-retry path), this is real money on no-op operations.

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: before invoking the
    reviewer, check the implementer outcome:
      - If outcome is no_changes (BaseRev == ResultRev and zero
        commits produced): skip reviewer entirely.
      - Route directly to the existing no_changes triage path
        (which classifies, applies labels, sets cooldowns or
        re-status as needed).
      - Emit a "reviewer-skipped-empty-diff" event so operators
        can see the optimization fired.
  - Preserve all existing no_changes handling logic — the only
    change is "don't pay the reviewer when there's nothing to
    review."
  - Edge case: review_spec_gap diagnosis happens at the IMPLEMENTER
    level (the implementer says "I can't because the spec is
    gappy"); the reviewer doesn't gate this. Confirm the no_changes
    triage path handles the spec-gap routing without reviewer.

  Tests:
     - TestNoChanges_SkipsReviewer: assert reviewer is NOT invoked
       when implementer outcome is no_changes.
     - TestSomeChanges_StillInvokesReviewer: assert reviewer IS
       invoked when implementer produced any commit.
     - TestNoChangesWithReviewSpecGapClassification_RoutesToProposed:
       assert the spec-gap path still routes to proposed without
       reviewer gating.
     - TestReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEvent: assert event is emitted.

NON-SCOPE
  - Skipping reviewer for non-no_changes outcomes (out of scope;
    those genuinely need review).
  - Tuning reviewer dispatch for partial-changes cases (out of
    scope).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-451cdead (ADR-024 amendment) — P1 cost
    optimization principle.
  - Sister bead ddx-cf79f10c (no_changes stepwise) — same
    no_changes path; this bead skips the reviewer cost; that
    bead handles the implementer escalation. Compose cleanly.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: when implementer returns no_changes (BaseRev == ResultRev and zero commits), reviewer dispatch is skipped.
2. The existing no_changes triage path handles routing; behavior is preserved (labels, cooldowns, status transitions match pre-change baseline).
3. A "reviewer-skipped-empty-diff" event is emitted on each skip so operators can see the optimization.
4. TestNoChanges_SkipsReviewer asserts reviewer is NOT invoked when outcome is no_changes.
5. TestSomeChanges_StillInvokesReviewer asserts reviewer IS invoked when implementer produced any commit.
6. TestNoChangesWithReviewSpecGapClassification_RoutesToProposed asserts spec-gap path still routes to proposed without reviewer gating.
7. TestReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEvent asserts the structured event is emitted.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, perf</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="a4b24af54651b89d4979dad70c4528ff5acb4ccf" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260511T013249-1c092fb4"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All done. The implementation is complete and all checks pass:

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`: Added `ReviewerSkippedEmptyDiffEventKind` constant, `emitReviewerSkippedEmptyDiff` helper, and replaced `runPostAttemptTriage` calls in the no_changes path with the new helper
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go`: Updated `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesStaysOpenAndContinues` to expect 4 events including the new `reviewer-skipped-empty-diff` event
- `cli/internal/agent/triage_hook_test.go`: Updated 5 existing tests to use `execution_failed` with different revs, added 4 new AC tests
- `cli/cmd/try_test.go`: Updated `TestTry_HooksWired` to reflect new behavior (only lint fires; `reviewer-skipped-empty-diff` event emitted instead of triage)
